html:
Exclusive to:

The B.U.D.D.Y. is a craftable Item exclusive to Don't Starve Together. It can only be crafted by players who have completed the radio puzzle approximately halfway through the Exspectamus Puzzles. Afterwards, it can be seen as an Unlock item in the Klei Account transactions menu. Once the B.U.D.D.Y. is unlocked, it can be crafted with 5 Scrap and 2 Electrical Doodads. It can then be placed, picked up and toggled freely by any other player, regardless of whether they have unlocked the item or not. Attempting to spawn the B.U.D.D.Y. with the c_give() command without first unlocking the item from the puzzle will cause the game to delete it from the world immediately.

Functionally, the B.U.D.D.Y. is very similar to a regular Gramophone. It is placed as a ground item rather than a structure, and will also tend to Farm Plants within a 2-tile radius. However, unlike the Gramophone, the same tune will play nonstop until manually deactivated (or destroyed), making it a very convenient tool for keeping crops maintained.

Activating the B.U.D.D.Y. will play the Exspectamus Puzzles' Gramophone song, which loops indefinitely until manually stopped:

The song cannot be heard if the music volume is set to zero in the game settings. The B.U.D.D.Y. can be heard within a 7-tile radius when standing in a straight line from it, and a 5-tile radius when standing diagonally from it.

Contents

  • 1 Skins
    • 1.1 B.U.D.D.Y. Parts
      • 1.1.1 Antenna
      • 1.1.2 Face
      • 1.1.3 Left
      • 1.1.4 Right
      • 1.1.5 Plate
  • 2 Trivia
  • 3 Gallery

Skins

The B.U.D.D.Y. does not have typical skins or rarities. Instead, its individual parts can be customized within the Exspectamus Puzzles website, from the screwdriver room. The radio parts available depend on the parts that have been located in the destroyed factory.

To find the screwdriver room:

  1. Move to the daytime factory section of the page.
  2. Scroll down to the desk, then click on the pencil to move to Wagstaff's desk.
  3. Click on the screwdriver found on the back shelf.

The radio's left side, face, right side, bottom plate, and antenna can all be swapped out with various pieces, by clicking on any of its parts.

When joining a world, the game server will only keep track of the first B.U.D.D.Y. skin that is set on the puzzle website; therefore, when changing skins, the player must then rejoin the world to apply it. The B.U.D.D.Y. skin that is currently set can be seen in the crafting menu. Any B.U.D.D.Y. crafted before switching the skin on the puzzle website will keep their former appearance; however, its skin can be changed to the currently set skin using the Clean Sweeper.

Trivia

  • B.U.D.D.Y. stands for "Broadcasting Uninterrupted Dimensional Dialogue Yet?"

Exclusive to:
B.U.D.D.Y.
"Where is that signal coming from?"
Object Info
Does not stack
Source
Can be Hammered
Crafting
×5 ×2
Filters
Tier
Always available
Miscellaneous
"w_radio"
“Hm. There's a lot of science here.”
–Wilson
“Ten seconds to make it work before I burn it.”
–Willow
“Hello little... what are you?”
–Wolfgang
“What does it do?”
–Wendy
“DO I KNOW YOU?”
–WX-78
“Interesting... I conclude it requires further study.”
–Wickerbottom
“Does it play any tunes?”
–Woodie
“It makes me uncomfortable.”
–Maxwell
“What infernal device is this?”
–Wigfrid
“Yay! New toy!”
–Webber
“What appliance is this?”
–Warly
“What friend do?”
–Wormwood
“I think I recognize this handywork...”
–Winona
“Oh dear, how'd that get here?”
–Wortox
“Make do stuff, flort!”
–Wurt
“What do you think it does, Woby?”
–Walter
“I don't trust it.”
–Wanda

It can only be crafted by players who have completed the radio puzzle approximately halfway through the [[Exspectamus Puzzles]]. Afterwards, it can be seen as an ''Unlock item'' in the [https://accounts.klei.com/account/transactions Klei Account transactions menu]. Once the B.U.D.D.Y. is unlocked, it can be crafted with 5 [[Scrap]] and 2 [[Electrical Doodad]]s. It can then be placed, picked up and toggled freely by any other player, regardless of whether they have unlocked the item or not. Attempting to spawn the B.U.D.D.Y. with the c_give() command without first unlocking the item from the puzzle will cause the game to delete it from the world immediately. Functionally, the B.U.D.D.Y. is very similar to a regular [[Gramophone]]. It is placed as a ground item rather than a structure, and will also tend to [[Farm Plant]]s within a 2-tile radius. However, unlike the Gramophone, the same tune will play nonstop until manually deactivated (or destroyed), making it a very convenient tool for keeping crops maintained. Activating the B.U.D.D.Y. will play the [[Exspectamus Puzzles#3rd bit (Pencil Room)|Exspectamus Puzzles' Gramophone song]], which loops indefinitely until manually stopped: [[File:Exspectamus Record.mp3]] The song cannot be heard if the music volume is set to zero in the game settings. The B.U.D.D.Y. can be heard within a 7-tile radius when standing in a straight line from it, and a 5-tile radius when standing diagonally from it. == [[File:Skins Icon.png|32px|link=]] Skins == The B.U.D.D.Y. does not have typical skins or rarities. Instead, its individual parts can be customized within the [[Exspectamus Puzzles]] website, from the screwdriver room. The radio parts available depend on the parts that have been located in the destroyed factory. To find the screwdriver room: # Move to the daytime factory section of the page. # Scroll down to the desk, then click on the pencil to move to Wagstaff's desk. # Click on the screwdriver found on the back shelf. The radio's left side, face, right side, bottom plate, and antenna can all be swapped out with various pieces, by clicking on any of its parts. When joining a world, the game server will only keep track of the first B.U.D.D.Y. skin that is set on the puzzle website; therefore, when changing skins, the player must then rejoin the world to apply it. The B.U.D.D.Y. skin that is currently set can be seen in the crafting menu. Any B.U.D.D.Y. crafted before switching the skin on the puzzle website will keep their former appearance; however, its skin can be changed to the currently set skin using the [[Clean Sweeper]]. === [[File:Exspectamus B.U.D.D.Y.
